title: knowledge.v1 — the engine's derived structural knowledge graph (committed entities)
description: The committed knowledge graph: one entity per engine surface-instance file (each schema, each check, each tool, and— as they appear — each contract, policy, conduct, operation, skill, agent, interface, and doc) plus one entity per installed module, with the mechanical edges between them. It is PURELY STRUCTURAL and PURELY DERIVED — it records how the engine is wired (which module owns a file, which schema governs it, what a check targets, which modules depend on which), never WHY a choice was made (belief lives in memory, not here). It is generated, never hand-authored, and a fingerprint coverage check keeps it matching the surfaces it is derived from. This schema governs the single committed file .engine/knowledge/graph.json: a version stamp plus the array of entities, each carrying its id, type, source file + content fingerprint, owning module, and a closed-vocabulary map of outgoing edges. The derived query index and graph-query server are separate, regenerable, gitignored layers shipped by a later slice; this committed file is the source of truth and the offline cold-start readout.

schema_version.description: The graph format's version stamp, so a future knowledge.v2 migration can recognise and transform an old graph. The only valid value is 1.

entities.description: Every entity in the graph, one per engine surface-instance file and one per installed module. Order is not significant (the generator sorts by id for a stable, reviewable diff).

entityId.description: An entity identifier: the surface name (or the literal 'module') followed by ':' and a slug. Stable and human-meaningful (e.g. 'check:state-cursor', 'schema:state.v1', 'module:core'); used as the join key a consumer hands to memory at read time.

entity.description: One node: a surface-instance file or an installed module, with its mechanically-derived facts and outgoing edges. No field carries belief — every value is read from the catalog, a manifest, a check's target, or the file's own bytes.

type.description: What this entity is: one of the eleven catalogued surface names, or 'module' for an installed module. Matches the prefix of the id.

name.description: A human-readable label for the entity — the repo-relative source path for a surface instance, the module id for a module.

slug.description: The id's suffix: the source file's stem (filename without its final extension) for a surface instance, or the module id for a module.

source.description: The canonical file this entity is derived from, with a content fingerprint so a change to that file without a regenerate is caught as drift.

path.description: The repo-relative path to the source file (forward slashes).

fingerprint.description: A sha256 of the source file's raw bytes, prefixed 'sha256:'. The per-entity drift signal.

owner.description: The id of the module that owns this entity's source file (the module whose 'provides' claims it); for a module entity, the module's own id.

status.description: The entity's declared lifecycle STATE TOKEN — never the why of a supersession. Harvested verbatim from a declared key for the two surfaces D-203 enumerates: a contract's frontmatter 'status' (proposed|accepted|superseded) and a module manifest's 'status' (required|default-on|optional|experimental|retired). Every other surface is 'active' (the 'else active' default; a surface's declared status is not echoed beyond those two). Optional only because a non-conforming source could omit it; the generator falls back to 'active'.

tier.description: CHECKS ONLY: the bite level declared in a check rule's 'tier' key (hard = fails the build; soft = warns). Absent on every non-check entity. This is the check rule's own tier, never a policy's prose enforcement tier (that is a '##' section and is not parsed).

title.description: An IDENTITY label harvested verbatim from a declared single-line bare-name key: policy.title, interface.title, or skill.name. A noun-phrase identity field only — never a description, never a slug fallback; omitted entirely when the surface declares none or the declared value fails the noun-phrase shape-guard. Excluded for operation/doc/contract surfaces (their titles are purpose/decision clauses, not identities).

kind.description: CHECK discriminator: the check-kind that evaluates this rule (e.g. schema|presence|shape|coverage|coherence|custom/script and module-provided kinds), from the check's 'kind' key. A free string mirroring check.v1; absent on non-check entities.

suites.description: CHECK discriminator: the validation suites this rule joins, from the check's 'suites' key, sorted. May be empty (a directly-invoked rule); absent on non-check entities.

role.description: AGENT discriminator: the persona's routing role, from the agent frontmatter 'role'. A free string (role membership is the coherence leg's, not enforced here); absent on non-agent entities.

lens.description: AGENT discriminator: the review lens, from the agent frontmatter 'lens'. Present only on review-role agents that declare one.

model-tier.description: AGENT discriminator: the execution-demand tier (e.g. judgment|mechanical), from the agent frontmatter 'model-tier'. A free string; absent on non-agent entities.

invocation.description: SKILL discriminator: the invocation axis (e.g. model-auto|operator-typed|model-only), from the skill frontmatter 'invocation'. A free string (membership is the coherence leg's); absent on non-skill entities.

operations.description: INTERFACE discriminator: the operation names the interface declares, from interface.operations[].name, sorted. Identity-only (the names, never the inline input/output schemas); absent on non-interface entities.

fallback.description: INTERFACE discriminator: the named fallback implementation handle, from interface.fallback.handle (the engine-prefixed server name). Absent on non-interface entities.

version.description: MODULE discriminator: the module manifest's 'version' (a dotted number, optionally a pre-release suffix). Absent on non-module entities.

predicates.description: The entity's OUTGOING edges, a closed mechanical vocabulary. Each value is an array of entity-ids that resolve to entities in this graph. Only non-empty edge kinds are present; an absent kind means no such edge. Reverse traversal (who governs/enforces/provides me) is computed by the derived index, not stored here.

provided_by.description: The module that owns this file (derived from the owning module's 'provides').

governed_by.description: The schema entity whose schema governs this file's shape (derived from the surface's catalog governing_schema; present only when that is an in-repo schema file, e.g. a check → schema:check.v1).

targets.description: For a check entity: the entities its target path selects (what the check validates). Derived from the check's target.path; file-path targets only.

depends_on.description: For a module entity: the modules it declares a dependency on (derived from the manifest's 'depends').

supersedes.description: For a contract entity: the earlier contract this decision replaces (forward, contract->contract), derived from the contract frontmatter's 'supersedes' eADR id resolved to its entity. Scoped to DEPLOYMENT-STREAM (non-canon) contracts only — a canon eADR (one a module's 'provides' claims) is NEVER a target, so no persisted reference reaches the canon (the why-of-a-supersession stays in the surface's own frontmatter, not here). Present only when this contract supersedes another in-graph deployment-stream contract.
